questions.query: How does reranking relate to retrieval quality?
questions.expected: Reranking is a post-fusion step that uses an LLM to reorder the top candidate chunks. By judging relevance directly it raises precision@k, at the cost of one extra LLM call per query.
key_facts: reranking adds an LLM call per query

questions.query: What is entity resolution for and how does it work here?
questions.expected: Entity resolution merges duplicate entity nodes (e.g. pgvector / PGVector) into one canonical node. It blocks candidates, scores them by string and embedding similarity, optionally asks an LLM on close calls, and records reversible merges.
key_facts: merges duplicate entities into a canonical node
